title: Retry storm amplifies a transient blip into an outage
summary: payment-svc has a brief transient 503 blip. checkout retries failed payments aggressively with no backoff, multiplying payment-svc load ~5x and driving it into sustained overload. The visible errors concentrate on payment-svc, but the lever is checkout's retry policy amplifying a transient. There is no recent deploy or config change in the window, so change-proximity reasoning alone is misleading.
question: Why is payment-svc returning sustained 503s since 14:41 on 2026-06-05?

ground_truth.notes: payment-svc is the amplified victim. The root lever is checkout's no-backoff retry policy. The naive suspect (payment-svc, where errors concentrate) must be ranked below the amplification hypothesis, and the absence of any in-window change must be reported, not ignored.
